parsed_review.summary: CP00-760 is a descriptor-only RP25 Migration Platform "primary service" closeout pack (40 units, risk B, range RP25.P02.M03.S01-RP25.P02.M04.S10). The pack correctly does NOT over-claim: manifest.json, construction-inspection.json, command-evidence.json, and adjudication.md all report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, pending exactly one valid hardened read-only Claude review (this receipt). Direct inspection of registry.js/service.js/validators.js/import-plan.js/mappers.js/index.js confirms descriptor-only, no-real-data, runtime-closed (file server/SharePoint/Drive/iManage/import), no-product-state-write, deny-over-allow, no-cross-tenant, and no-audit-event-body guards; a targeted scan found none of these risk flags set to true in either source or the generated contract. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, Claude packets read_only=true / promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes packets are deterministic evidence-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Deliverable distribution (contract2/impl20/security_audit4/ui4/claude_review2/failure_recovery2/test4/fixture1/hermes_evidence1=40) and micro-title split (30/10) reconcile with the unit list and CP760_REQUIREMENTS. Captured evidence is internally consistent: all validation commands exit 0 and the model test suite reports 16/16 passing in TAP output; the migration contract is regenerated with CP00-760 as latest projection and validation.valid=true; all referenced npm scripts exist. Provenance: per the read-only constraint I did not re-execute commands — I verified the captured command-evidence is consistent with the source, manifest plan-binding snapshot, and regenerated contract I read directly. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3 maintainability note (per-pack validator/descriptor duplication). Recommend PASS so the pack can proceed to adjudication and closeout.

findings.title: Per-pack coverage/descriptor validators are duplicated across CP756-CP760
findings.details: The five validateMigrationPlatform*Coverage and validateMigrationPlatform*Descriptor functions are near-identical (~40 lines each), differing only by binding, requirements, and the case-set field name; the create*Descriptor/Hermes/Claude/Handoff families in service.js follow the same copy-per-pack shape. This duplication grows linearly with each RP25 pack and increases drift risk over time. There is no correctness impact for CP00-760: all required checks (descriptor-only, runtime-closed, no sensitive payloads, deny-over-allow, no cross-tenant, no audit body, Hermes/Claude gate, handoff binding, authority boundary) are present and uniformly enforced, and the 16/16 test suite exercises them.
findings.recommendation: Optionally parameterize the shared coverage/descriptor validation into a single factory keyed by binding+requirements (and similarly for the service builders) to reduce future drift. Non-blocking; safe to defer.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as-is; non-blocking. Duplication appears intentional for per-pack auditability and does not affect descriptor-only/no-write guarantees, authority boundaries, or test coverage. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
